items.title: Come a day early
items.body: Coming from far away? Thursday is the gentlest day to arrive. Settle into your hotel and find dinner at a country tavern before everyone else gets in. A few of our favorites are on the [Travel](travel.html) page.

items.title: Welcome drinks
items.body: A glass of something cold to begin the evening. Find a familiar face, meet the half of the room you haven’t met yet, and let the evening open slowly.

items.title: The ceremony
items.body: The reason we’re all here. Please be seated by a quarter to five; it will be short, and entirely meant.

items.title: Cocktails & the golden hour
items.body: Drinks, small bites, and the best light of the day.

items.title: Dinner & dancing
items.where: The barn · seated dinner, then the floor
items.body: A long, generous dinner, a few toasts, and a dance floor we fully intend to wear out. Dress code: **garden formal** — suits and floor- or cocktail-length dresses. Much of the evening is on grass, so flats or block heels will serve you better than stilettos.

items.title: The send-off
items.body: A last song, a proper send-off, and a shuttle back to the hotels for anyone who’d like one.

items.title: Farewell breakfast
items.body: Coffee, pastries, and an unhurried morning before the drive home. No agenda — just one more cup together before everyone heads off.
